page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 40 MB L3 cache. This instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance is positioned for low-intensity workloads. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance scored 950.58, placing it in the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance reached 3679.45. Memory bandwidth tests show 30.12 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 22.11 GB/sec for write operations. Cryptographic and compression tasks also reflect entry-level capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 5065.1 and a Redis SET performance of 5.32 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.e4.xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio on a shared CPU allocation model. This makes it a budget-friendly option for memory-intensive tasks that do not require sustained, high-performance compute resources. The primary tradeoff is the reliance on older Intel Xeon hardware and shared CPU resources, which limits its suitability for compute-bound or latency-sensitive applications.

This instance is best suited for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. Appropriate use cases include hosting medium-sized caching layers, running lightweight database operations, serving static web content, or executing basic background tasks that benefit from 32 GB of RAM but do not require intensive CPU processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor at 2.5 GHz under the KVM hypervisor. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data shows entry-level performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 950.58 and a multi-core score of 3679.45. Memory bandwidth is measured at 30.12 GB/sec for large-block reads and 22.11 GB/sec for writes. Operating with a shared CPU allocation and a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, the ecs.e4.xlarge provides a cost-effective option for memory-heavy applications with low compute requirements. It is well-suited for caching layers, lightweight database operations, and static web serving.

tagline: A budget-friendly, memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for low-intensity database and caching workloads.

Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor at 2.5 GHz
Features a shared CPU allocation model under the KVM hypervisor
